name: Fake Social Proof
summary: Fabricated popularity signals — fake '17 people viewing', manufactured testimonials, bot-generated reviews.

evidence.claim: Fake social proof is listed in Brignull's canonical deceptive-patterns taxonomy.
FTC issued a final Rule on the Use of Consumer Reviews and Testimonials (16 CFR Part 465) effective October 21 2024, which prohibits fake reviews and testimonials.

body.description: Fabricated popularity signals: '17 people are viewing this', 'Sarah from Ohio just bought this', undisclosed incentivized reviews, or reviews with temporal/linguistic homogeneity indicating bulk generation (E1). Fake reviews explicitly prohibited by the FTC Fake Reviews Rule effective October 2024 (E2).

detectionHeuristics.description: Reviews posted within narrow windows, or reviews sharing distinctive phrasing, indicate potential fabrication.

body.llmVerifyPrompt: Given the page's social-proof elements and (if provided) the review corpus, assess: (1) Are viewer/buyer notifications verifiable or likely theatrical? (2) Do reviews cluster temporally in a way indicating bulk generation? (3) Are there language-homogeneity signals? Return {fakeSocialProofScore: 0..1, signalsFound: []}.
body.remediation: Warn the user. For reviews specifically, trigger Workflow 17 (review authenticity analysis) for a detailed verdict.
